name: Claire Music
occupation: Night security guard at a nearby building
background: Works as a night guard at an office building 200 meters from Belmont Fine Jewelers. Has been doing this job for 3 years. Clean record, no criminal history. Was on shift the night of March 15. She was seen on the street by a CCTV camera at 2:35 AM during her routine round — which placed her near the jewelry store just before the break-in. Police brought her in because of this proximity.
personality: Calm, polite, and cooperative. Genuinely wants to help the investigation. Answers questions directly and clearly. A bit surprised to be questioned but not rattled by it. Straightforward and consistent in her statements.
hidden_truth: Claire is completely innocent. She was just doing her nightly patrol round when she walked past the jewelry store. She did NOT see anything unusual at the store itself because the break-in started about 12 minutes after she passed by. She clearly remembers a white van parked on Rue du Louvre, about 50 meters from the jewelry store. The van had its engine running and parking lights on. She noticed a partial license plate: it started with 'AB-3' something. She also saw a man sitting in the driver seat — he was wearing a dark hoodie and seemed to be waiting for someone. She thought it was a delivery driver on a break, which is common in the area at night. She went back inside her building by 2:40 AM and stayed there the rest of the night. Her badge logs confirm entry at 2:41 AM. Her colleague David, the receptionist, saw her come back in.
strategy: Tell the truth fully and cooperate. She has nothing to hide and she knows it. She proactively shares every detail she can remember: the white van on Rue du Louvre with engine running, the partial plate starting with 'AB-3', the man in the dark hoodie in the driver seat. She volunteers this information without being asked. She offers badge logs, her patrol schedule, and her colleague David as a witness. She gives precise times and locations. She stays composed and patient even if the inspector pushes hard. She genuinely wants to help solve the case.
vulnerabilities: She was physically near the store at the time of the crime
She did not notice the break-in because it started 12 minutes after she passed by
